title: The Dead Village
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 2's final approach to Millhaven.
openLocation: Millhaven, just south of the Quel'Thalas borderlands.
transport: Mounted approach on Thalassian horses; on foot during the village sweep.
immediateObjective: Read Millhaven, find out what happened to its people, and decide whether the danger is really moving toward Quel'Thalas.
constraints: No thaw or trust-language overstatement yet — Jaina becomes necessary read, not emotional comfort.
No grain confirmation, organized cult proof, or recognition of the old trader as anything but a local source.

povKnowsAtOpen: The contamination is real, physical, and big enough to matter beyond one village.
Jaina's readings are useful even when her sense of the road is poor.
Millhaven is the first place on the south road where the wrongness knots tight.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How it first got into the village.
How large the threat is beyond this village.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No assumption that the old trader is anything but a local source.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Millhaven is the first fully claimed village on this road.
The survivor girl's testimony plus the follow-up tracking pass confirm the dead marched north toward Quel'Thalas.
Neral rides north with the girl and warning, leaving only Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel to keep riding south.
The newer grain-seller mention enters the file as a weak unresolved clue.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: This is still border defense to read, report, and answer, not a human catastrophe Sylvanas has to own on its own terms.
pov.surfaceObjective: Read Millhaven cleanly enough to turn shock into a warning she can send home before the road worsens again.
pov.pressureSource: The village is not a rumor or a bad patrol report. It is a whole settlement gone wrong, with missing dead, one living witness, and a kind of wrongness Sylvanas cannot price without Jaina.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas keeps treating Jaina as a specialist she can consult when needed, not as half of the field read.
pov.chapterTest: Millhaven forces her to weigh tracks, corpses, silence, witness testimony, and magic together. None of it resolves cleanly if she cuts out Jaina's side.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep Jaina on a short leash as a useful prisoner, or start letting her read shape what the party does next.
pov.endStateShift: By chapter close Sylvanas still does not trust Jaina, but she stops treating the mage's read as optional color. It is part of the map now.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 4 opens with Sylvanas expecting Jaina to speak when the wrongness turns strange, even if authority still stays in Sylvanas's hands.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The only woman here who can tell how deep the wrongness goes and which parts of it matter.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Useful, but still a human mage who might see the magical half more clearly than the ground under their boots.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Jaina does not just name the wrongness. She helps separate what is loud from what matters, which keeps the field read from collapsing into blind horror.
throughPov.interactionRule: Sylvanas still withholds warmth. Jaina earns more room to speak, not comfort.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Custody ride under pressure. Useful prisoner, armed escort, no trust.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The village makes side-by-side reading unavoidable. By the end, they are building one picture out of two kinds of evidence.
team.namingAttitudeRule: No easy softening yet. If Sylvanas uses Jaina's name in her own head, it should land because the mage's read is doing real work, not because the mood warmed.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This is first proof of usefulness, not rapport.

entries.rule: Remains with the party and is the ranger Sylvanas trusts most on the ground.
Stays with the party into Chapter 4.
Present at chapter open. She stays through the cellar rescue and the follow-up tracking pass, then rides north with the survivor and warning dispatch once the dead's direction is confirmed.
Present at chapter open as the spare fourth rider from Chapter 2. Leaves north with Neral and the survivor so the southbound group reduces cleanly to the four named leads.
Found in a cellar this chapter. She does not continue south with Sylvanas and Jaina; Neral escorts her north on horseback once the dispatch leaves.
First Mal'Ganis disguise. Must read as useful, local, unremarkable, and not yet suspicious to the POV.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 4 opens after a two-day southbound move. By then the traveling party is Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel only.

scenes.title: Millhaven
scenes.sceneFunction: Show the first village the plague has truly taken, and make the missing dead a real problem.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The contamination is real and physical.
How it got here is still unclear.
scenes.arcPressure: The first empty village forces Sylvanas to admit this is no longer a border anomaly she can classify from a saddle. If the dead are missing, the threat is already mobile.

enrichment.detail: Border-country villages south of Quel'Thalas should feel rougher and more agricultural than Eversong: muddy lanes, livestock trace, woodsmoke residue, autumn-harvest wear, and river-working buildings instead of polished elven quiet.
Millhaven's first impression should stay environmental before forensic: river bend, still mill wheel, squat granary, cold chimneys, and no answering dog or lantern.

beats.goal: Enter the village carefully and assess whether it is occupied, trapped, or abandoned.
beats.conflict: Millhaven is intact but acoustically dead; every normal village sound is missing.

outcome.summary: The party gains entry, but the absence itself is worse than visible damage.

newOnPageInformation: Millhaven was not burned, sacked, or visibly fought over.
The wrongness is recent enough that ordinary life still seems interrupted rather than erased.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Where the people are.
Whether anything inside is still living.
sensoryAnchors: cold stove
swinging door
no livestock noise
dust on places that should have been touched today
horses reacting to the silence
beats.transitionIntent: sweep-pressure: Sylvanas orders a real physical search instead of leaving the whole village to Jaina's magic.

beats.goal: Read the village for timeline and population fate.
beats.conflict: Every building says 'left in the middle of living' while offering no bodies and no obvious kill site.

outcome.summary: The sweep produces evidence of staggered collapse but no explanation for the vanished population.

newOnPageInformation: The collapse took days, not a single sudden event.
Domestic life froze in place before emptying out.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the missing population fled, was taken, or rose.
sensoryAnchors: food gone stale on tables
child's toy left where it fell
cat as the only casual living presence
beats.transitionIntent: verification-pressure: Jaina tests the contamination inside the most saturated structures.

beats.goal: Let Jaina test whether the village gives up how this started.
beats.conflict: The whole place reads wrong: well, soil, root cellar, granary, trough.

outcome.summary: Jaina proves the whole village is soaked in it, but she still cannot say what first brought it in.

beats.valueShift: impossible absence -> the whole village is soaked, but the cause is still slippery
newOnPageInformation: The granary is the strongest single read, but Jaina refuses to mistake the loudest layer for the whole answer.
Sylvanas is starting to treat Jaina as the one kind of reader she does not have.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether it came through grain, water, soil, or some slower layering.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina kneeling in grain while Sylvanas clears the room behind her
normal-looking stores with invisible wrongness
stale dust and closed-space heat
beats.transitionIntent: ground-proof: Sylvanas takes the search outside and puts her hands on the ground herself.

beats.goal: Determine what happened to the bodies.
beats.conflict: The ground behind the village is too churned and partial to call cleanly; there are drag marks, broken crust, and trampled exits, but not yet a direction Sylvanas will stake a border warning on.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas confirms many of the dead left Millhaven on their feet, but the ground alone does not yet prove where they went.

beats.valueShift: the whole village is soaked, but the cause is still slippery -> too much danger, not enough proof
newOnPageInformation: A large number of bodies are missing from the village.
The dead did not simply collapse in place and stay there.
Sylvanas needs either a witness or a cleaner line of spoor before she commits a warning north.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the dead moved toward Quel'Thalas or elsewhere.
Who is directing them.
Where the rest of the villagers are.
sensoryAnchors: broken dirt where too many feet dragged through the same lane
Sylvanas crouched over muddied sign she refuses to overcall
Cyndia frustrated by counts without a trustworthy line
beats.transitionIntent: witness-pressure: the ground is not enough; the chapter now needs a living witness or cleaner evidence.

scenes.title: The Survivor and the Proof
scenes.sceneFunction: Put a living child inside the disaster, then use her to turn guesswork into warning.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The ground alone has not yet proved where the dead went.
scenes.arcPressure: A living witness can turn disgust into proof, but only if Sylvanas lets Jaina's read and a terrified child's fragments count together instead of trusting only what the ground says cleanly.

enrichment.detail: Canon plague spread in Lordaeron through infected food supplies; early civilian experience would surface as sickness moving house to house, deaths within days, and the dead rising soon after, so the girl's remembered sweetness and staggered collapse are good witness texture without proving grain yet.

Lordaeron's ordinary life should still feel recently present in the houses and cellar spaces: the horror is not ruin but interruption, which keeps the survivor rescue from reading generically post-apocalyptic.

beats.goal: Find any living witness left in Millhaven.
beats.conflict: They locate an alive but traumatized girl hidden in a cellar, dehydrated, terrified, and unreliable in sequence.

outcome.summary: They get a witness, but the testimony is fragmentary and emotionally broken.

beats.valueShift: mobilization risk without proof -> living testimony at last
newOnPageInformation: At least one living person survived inside the village.
Neral is the first ranger the girl does not recoil from, which makes her the natural escort if the child has to be moved.
The girl confirms death, rising, and night movement rather than simple disease collapse.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Exact timeline.
Direction of the dead.
How it first got in.
sensoryAnchors: cellar damp
child's whisper gone hoarse
Jaina lowering herself into the dark while Sylvanas controls the space above
Neral crouching to the girl's height instead of looming over her
beats.transitionIntent: extraction-pressure: the girl can talk, but only in broken pieces, so the party must pull signal from trauma without pretending she is reliable.

beats.goal: Pull usable intelligence from the survivor.
beats.conflict: The girl remembers in images, not order: sweetness on people's breath, neighbors getting sick, bodies rising, dead shuffling out toward the north road at night, adults telling children to stay hidden, and complaint about a newer grain man on the route.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gains confirmation that the dead rose and moved with direction, and hears one early oddity in who had been selling grain, but the child's memory still needs the ground to back it.

beats.valueShift: living testimony at last -> horror with direction implied
newOnPageInformation: Some risen dead came back for the sick who remained.
The girl remembers movement toward the north road rather than blind milling.
There was talk of a newer grain seller before the collapse.
The girl is too unstable to build a full timeline from alone.
Jaina is the one who can pull the few useful pieces out of the girl's broken story, which Sylvanas notices.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the northbound memory matches the physical trail cleanly enough to warn Quel'Thalas.
Whether the grain clue matters or is incidental.
sensoryAnchors: girl's fingers locked around a blanket scrap
Jaina hearing the tremor in every remembered detail
Neral keeping the girl steady with quiet, practical questions instead of orders
beats.transitionIntent: corroboration-pressure: the testimony points north, but Sylvanas still needs the ground to agree before she spends riders on warning.

beats.goal: Verify whether the girl's broken memory matches the ground outside Millhaven.
beats.conflict: The road cut north of the village is damaged by wagon ruts, scavengers, and too many overlapping exits, so the read has to be made from scraps instead of a clean trail.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas and Cyndia finally confirm a north-northeast mass movement toward Quel'Thalas, and the answer immediately widens the strategic emergency.

beats.valueShift: horror with direction implied -> the dead marched toward home
newOnPageInformation: The girl's testimony and the physical spoor now support each other.
At least sixty reanimated bodies moved north in rough formation.
No idea what prompted it.
Jaina's magical read helps strip ordinary road damage out of the spoor, which Sylvanas files as another kind of tracking whether she likes the comparison or not.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the dead are bunching up on their own or being pushed into line by living hands.
Whether the warning will arrive in time to matter.
sensoryAnchors: parallel grooves finally clean enough to trust at the north road cut
Sylvanas reading the ground with her fingertips and committing to the call
Cyndia's count turning from doubt into alarm
beats.transitionIntent: dispatch-pressure: the proof is finally good enough to justify a warning north, and the survivor now has to leave with it.

scenes.title: The Grain Seller on the Road
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn proof into action, bring in the old trader as a useful local, send Neral north with the girl, and close on the smaller group riding south.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The girl and the ground together have confirmed the dead are moving north.
The child cannot stay with the investigation.
Neral is the obvious dispatch rider.
"New grain man" exists only as one witness fragment.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas has to weaken her escort to get warning north while deciding how much weight to give Jaina's read, a frightened child's clue, and a stranger's road knowledge without losing control of the investigation.

enrichment.detail: Farstriders are the 'eyes and blades' of Quel'Thalas and are built to meet danger before it reaches the gates, which supports a clean split here: warning north, smaller investigation unit south.

The Lordaeron plague moved through ordinary grain distribution out of Andorhal, so an empty wagon with grain dust in the slats is canon-consistent road texture even while the chapter keeps grain only as an unresolved clue.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Plague of Undeath; local ref: stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md

enrichment.detail: Mal'Ganis canonically commands plague operations in Lordaeron and later successfully uses an unremarkable mortal guise; if the old trader is his first mask here, keep him practical, forgettable, and locally convincing rather than ominous on the nose.

beats.emotion: Urgency with no time to indulge it.
beats.dilemma: Keep Neral for the road south, or cut her loose now so Quel'Thalas gets warning and the child gets out.
beats.decision: Put the warning in Neral's hands, send her north with the survivor and the unnamed escort ranger, and keep Cyndia and Lyriel on the southbound ride.
beats.valueShift: the dead marched toward home -> one rider gone, danger larger
newOnPageInformation: Neral is the best rider to carry a warning north in a hurry.
Neral is unexpectedly good with frightened children, which makes the choice cleaner than a purely tactical handoff.
The unnamed fourth rider folds into Neral's northbound escort, which leaves the southbound group at the four named leads.
Sylvanas is prioritizing the border threat even while continuing south.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the warning will arrive in time to matter.
sensoryAnchors: written message sealed in haste
dispatch tube strapped at Neral's saddle
Neral calming the girl with matter-of-fact talk about safe stables, sweet feed, and the horned white 'unicorn' mounts children in Quel'Thalas grow up dreaming about
beats.transitionIntent: road-contact: the decision is made; one last local exchange can still sharpen the picture before the party splits directions.

beats.goal: Gather route intelligence and see how far the outbreak has progressed beyond Millhaven.
beats.conflict: An old grain seller on the road north has exactly the kind of unforced local knowledge a field commander values, and finding Millhaven totally gone catches him visibly off guard instead of confirming anything he expected.

outcome.summary: He gives useful road talk, and his surprise makes it clear Millhaven shocked him too, but the party still has to trust a stranger's read of the country ahead.

beats.valueShift: one rider gone, danger larger -> the road picture gets clearer
newOnPageInformation: Settlements ahead are still trading and only lightly touched by sickness.
Millhaven is the first village on this road fully claimed by the plague.
The trader's empty wagon is heading north, and he clearly did not expect to find the village silent.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: That the trader is linked to the contamination itself.
sensoryAnchors: grain dust in the slats of the empty wagon
surprise breaking through his otherwise forgettable face
girl settling in front of Neral's saddle while the horse sidesteps
beats.transitionIntent: split-pressure: the exchange sharpens the road picture just before the party commits to opposite directions.
beats.plantedThread: The old trader's road-farewell cadence and grain-wagon texture should feel specific enough to recur later without reading suspicious now.

beats.goal: Complete the parting cleanly and move on.
beats.conflict: The trader's farewell lands just oddly enough to earn Sylvanas a brief glance back, but the moment is too thin to matter more than the dead and the road south.

outcome.summary: The parting completes cleanly, though Sylvanas gives him one second look before letting the matter go.

beats.valueShift: the road picture gets clearer -> parting complete, eyes south again
newOnPageInformation: The survivor girl leaves the chapter here under Neral's escort, headed north on horseback.
Sylvanas gives the trader a brief look back before turning south again.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: The trader's real identity.
Whether the trader matters at all beyond this practical handoff.
sensoryAnchors: Sylvanas's brief glance back
wagon wheels starting north again after that startled pause
girl silent against Neral's saddle as the horse steps out
beats.transitionIntent: reduced-party: with Neral escorting the girl north, the road south narrows to Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel.

beats.dilemma: Turn back after warning the border, or keep following the stronger readings and the live-settlement trail.
beats.decision: Keep moving south toward the next still-living village.
beats.valueShift: parting complete, eyes south again -> committed pursuit
newOnPageInformation: Only Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel are riding south now.
They are committing to a multi-day push deeper into Lordaeron.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Grain as the thing carrying it.
The organization behind the plague.
sensoryAnchors: road opening into broader farmland
stronger readings ahead
one fewer escort horse in the party after Neral's dispatch
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-hook: Chapter 4 opens two days later at the next village, still alive and still misunderstanding what is happening to it.

departuresLocked: Neral is sent north with the warning dispatch and the survivor girl.
The unnamed escort ranger goes north with Neral and the survivor girl.
The survivor girl is headed north under Neral's escort on horseback.
factsLockedForChapter4: Millhaven is dead, and the survivor girl's testimony plus the follow-up tracking pass confirmed its dead marched toward Quel'Thalas.
Millhaven appears to be the first village on this road fully claimed; settlements beyond are still sick enough to hide the pattern from ordinary travelers.
There was one survivor girl in a cellar.
Neral escorted that girl north with the warning dispatch on horseback.
The party riding into Chapter 4 is only Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel.
They have heard 'new grain seller' once, but do not yet know grain is what carries it.
factsNotYetEarned: Recognition of the trader as anything but a helpful local.

warnings: Beats 1-7 run seven consecutive scene beats before the sequel at beat 8. Keep the village-read and cellar sections sharply differentiated so escalation does not flatten.
Beats 1-4 all darken the chapter in the same downward direction as Millhaven goes from silence to saturation to missing dead. Vary the prose texture so each reveal does different work.
Scene outcomes skew entirely toward yes-but / no-and. The pressure is strong, but the chapter will need careful modulation so every gain does not feel blunted in the same way.
